title: holcus lanatus var. argenteus
intro: Holcus lanatus var. argenteus, also known as silver bent or silver cock's-foot, is a variation of the common meadow grass Holcus lanatus, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of silver bent include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as a natural diuretic. Silver bent can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used in traditional folk medicine to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
